subtasks.name: PHASE 1 — Research & Direction
subtasks.description: Initial research, visual exploration, and direction-setting phase focused on establishing the overall UX/UI foundation and creative approach for the project before entering the main design production process.

subtasks.name: 1.1 - Sector & Competitor Research
subtasks.description: Initial visual and UX research focused on competitor products, industry standards, interface trends, and relevant app experiences before starting the design process. This task is intended to support internal design direction, inspiration gathering, and overall product positioning rather than producing a formal research presentation or deep analytical report.

subtasks.name: 1.2 - Mood Board & Visual Direction
subtasks.description: Visual exploration and creative direction development based on the research phase and overall product goals. This process may include mood boards, style references, interface experiments, and early visual direction studies to help establish a suitable design language for the product. Depending on the workflow and project needs, selected visual directions or references may be shared during the process when necessary.

subtasks.name: 1.3 - Initial Design Concept Definition
subtasks.description: Selection and refinement of an initial visual direction based on research, explorations, and early design experiments conducted during the first stages of the project. This task focuses on narrowing down the overall design direction and establishing a clearer foundation for the upcoming UI design process before moving into the initial design proposal phase.

subtasks.name: PHASE 2 — Initial Design Proposals
subtasks.description: First round of design concepts presented to the client. Two alternative directions explored; one selected and refined after client feedback.

subtasks.name: 2.1 - Initial Design Proposal
subtasks.description:
Full design concept applied to key screens (Home and potentially one additional screen). Presents a distinct visual language, color palette, and component style.

This screen will also include navigation and main page layout design solutions.

subtasks.name: 2.2 - [REVISION 1] Client Feedback & Iterations
subtasks.description: Collect client feedback, align on the chosen direction, and document all change requests. Output: Approved concept brief + revision notes.

subtasks.name: 2.3 - Concept Improvements
subtasks.description: Apply revision notes to the selected concept. Refine typography, spacing, colour, and component style until the design language is locked and approved. Apply the new design approach to one or more additional pages to evaluate its results and refine design decisions as needed before beginning the full app design process.

subtasks.name: PHASE 3 — UX Analysis & Screen Solutions
subtasks.description: Deep-dive into every app screen to identify UX problems and define layout and component solutions before full visual design begins.

subtasks.name: 3.1 - Iterative UX Audit, Improvements & Solutions (Screen-Level)
subtasks.description: Ongoing UX analysis, usability improvements, and interaction refinements performed throughout the design process on a screen-by-screen basis. This task includes identifying UX opportunities during UI production, exploring better interface behaviors, refining user flows, and applying practical UX solutions iteratively as the product design evolves.

subtasks.name: PHASE 4 — Design System & Core Branding Updates
subtasks.description: Full brand and design system work: logo, branding renewal, component library, icon set and AI-assisted imagery.

subtasks.name: 4.1 - Wordmark & Emblem Rework
subtasks.description:
Refinement and modernization of the existing wordmark and emblem to achieve a cleaner, more contemporary, and stronger visual identity. The current approach focuses on improving the overall visual quality through typography refinement, balanced proportions, and better brand presence rather than conducting a deep full-scale branding process.

An existing emblem concept has previously been developed and may provide a stronger and more recognizable direction for app-related usage. Integrating and refining the emblem for mobile app environments, alongside improvements to the current wordmark, could help establish a more cohesive and scalable brand presence across the product ecosystem.

subtasks.name: 4.2 - App Icon Design
subtasks.description: Adaptation and refinement of the existing FPV emblem into a mobile app icon format. This task includes creating a simple, clean, and visually strong app icon aligned with the current FPV brand identity. If necessary, the process may include limited visual explorations and iterative refinements to evaluate different app icon directions. However, the current goal is to achieve a simple but polished and effective app icon solution rather than conducting a large-scale icon exploration process.

subtasks.name: 4.3 - Typography solutions
subtasks.description: Typography exploration and refinement for the overall app experience, including researchand selection of suitable typefaces for headlines, base texts, numeric usage,and interface readability. This task also includes defining typography hierarchy, sizing, weights, spacing, and related style guide values to establish a clean, scalable, and visually cohesive typography system throughout the product.

subtasks.name: 4.5 - Icon Set
subtasks.description: Preparing consistent icon set covering all functional icons used across the app: navigation, actions, status indicators, and service category icons.

subtasks.name: 4.6 - Design System Setup — Tokens, Components & Styles
subtasks.description: Build the full design system: colour tokens, type scale, spacing scale, elevation, grid, and all reusable UI components (buttons, cards, inputs, modals, badges, nav bars etc.).

subtasks.name: PHASE 5 — Full App Design
subtasks.description: Complete screen-by-screen breakdown of the existing FPV mobile app. Covers all pages, sub-pages, modals, fields, and navigation flows as analysed from app screenshots.

subtasks.name: 5.1 - Authentication & Onboarding
subtasks.description: Splash, welcome, Login, registration, and password recovery screens for the FPV client portal.

subtasks.name: 5.1.1 - Client Portal Login Screen
subtasks.description: Email & password login form. Fields: Email Address, Password, Forgot Password link. Actions: Login button, Sign in with Google, Sign in with Apple, Create Account link.

subtasks.name: 5.1.2 - Client Portal Registration Modal
subtasks.description: New account creation form. Fields: First Name, Last Name, Mobile Phone, Broker Email, Password, Confirm Password, Real Estate Brokerage Agency Name, State Licensure ID. NMI Payment Terms checkbox consent.

subtasks.name: 5.1.3 - Forgot Password / Credentials Reset Wizard
subtasks.description: Step 1: Email input + Send Recovery Link. Step 2: 6-digit verification code input. Step 3: New Password + Confirm Password + Reset Password button.

subtasks.name: 5.2 - Home Page (Dashboard)
subtasks.description: Main app dashboard with order summaries, upcoming appointments, and notification alerts.

subtasks.name: 5.2.1 - Main Dashboard Layout Solution/s
subtasks.description: App header (Profile icon, FPV logo, Notification bell badge). Welcome Card (T. My Orders Summary Grid: Drafts, Pending Scan, Processing, Completed counters. Active Orders panel with order cards (#DEMO-001). Upcoming Appointments card. FPV Promotional Banner.

subtasks.name: 5.2.2 - My Orders Module
subtasks.description: Detailed order status screens with logistics accordion drawers for active, scheduled, and completed projects.

subtasks.name: 5.2.3 - Notifications Screen / Alerts Feed
subtasks.description: Header: Back arrow + Notifications title. Alert feed cards showing: Scheduling alerts, Invoice billing alerts, Blueprint completion alerts. Read vs Unread visual indicators.

subtasks.name: 5.3 - Account Page
subtasks.description: Full user account management: profile editing, orders history, autofill defaults, wallet, favorites, and feedback hub.

subtasks.name: 5.3.1 - Account Bar
subtasks.description: Profile identity card (Taylor Morgan, Morgan Realty). Grid options: Edit Profile, My Orders, AutoFill Preferences, Payment Methods/Wallet, Favorites, Feedback Hub. Logout button (red).

subtasks.name: 5.3.1.A - Profile Edit Modal
subtasks.description: Editable form fields: First Name, Last Name, Phone Number, Email Address, Company Name. Company Account toggle switch (agency-wide sharing). Actions: Save Changes, Cancel.

subtasks.name: 5.3.2.A - Orders
subtasks.description: Filter tabs: Active (scheduled/processing) and Past (completed/archived). Order cards showing: Order ID, Date, Address, Service Badges (Plans/CAD/Matterport), Status Badges (Awaiting/Completed/Cancelled).

subtasks.name: 5.3.3 - AutoFill Preferences
subtasks.description: Default POC section: Same as Account Info toggle, Name/Phone/Email fields. Blueprint Deliverables defaults: PDF format, AutoCAD version selector. Lot & Formatting: Dimension Lines, Area Sizing, Compass orientation radio (True North vs Door), Color scheme (B&W vs Color).

subtasks.name: 5.3.4 - Wallet
subtasks.description: PCI/NMI security banner. Saved cards list (Visa **** 4242). Add Payment Method button. Sub-modal: Add Secure Payment Method with Cardholder Name, PAN Number, Expiry MM/YY, CVV, Set as Default toggle.

subtasks.name: 5.3.5 - Favorites (Content is unknown)
subtasks.description: Pinned properties registry with repeat addresses, saved technician lockbox notes, and saved search criteria. Execute Repeat Order instant shortcut.

subtasks.name: 5.3.7 - Beta Feedback Hub
subtasks.description: Ticket log feed with status badges (Open, In Review, Resolved). Developer reply threads. Submit Feedback button. Sub-modal: Subject, Description text area, Category dropdown (Bug/Suggestion/UI/Other), Attach Screenshot action.

subtasks.name: 5.4 - Orders
subtasks.description: Order creation flow: selection hub dispatcher, 11-step residential wizard, and search inventory checkout flow.

subtasks.name: 5.4.2.A - Order Selection Main Screen
subtasks.description: 4 order type cards: Marketable Plan (Active, launches wizard), As-Built Plan (Coming Soon), 3D Renderings (Coming Soon), Site Plans (Coming Soon). Locked feature overlay modal with Coming Soon message.

subtasks.name: 5.4.2.B - Marketable Plan Residential & Wizard (11-Step Flow)
subtasks.description:
Full 11-step order form. 

Step 1: Contact Info — Name, phone, email, and brokerage.

Step 2: Property Address — Google Places API autocomplete with street, city, state, and ZIP fields.

Step 3: Level & Structure — Story count, basement (Y/N — finished/unfinished), attic (Y/N), and detached structures.

Step 4: Matterport Details — Include toggle, interior + exterior capture, dollhouse view, measurement tool, and 3-month hosting.

Step 5: Compass Orientation — True North vs. Front Door.

Steps 6–9: Reserved custom spec slots.

Step 10: Credit Checkout — Cardholder name, PAN, MM/YY, CVV, and NMI terms acceptance.

Step 11: Success Confirmation — Order number, email receipt notification, and link to Active Order.

subtasks.name: 5.4.2.C - (Missing) Marketable Plans for Commercial / Mizxed Use
5.4.2.E - (Missing) Remodeling Plans for Commercial / Mixed Use
5.4.2.F - (Missing) Purchae and Rebrand Existing FPV Plan
5.4.2.H - Search Inventory & Checkout Flow (3 sub pages)
subtasks.description:
Page 01: Search Box Console: Address/Property ID field with State, City, Zip, Sq Ft, and CAD-available filters.

Page 02: Results Page: Property cards displaying a thumbnail, square footage, CAD badge, price, and a Select & Checkout button.

Page 03: Tab Checkout Wizard:
Tab 1 – Account Info: Same As Account toggle, POC fields, NMI consent
Tab 2 – Order Info: Address validation, Relationship to Property dropdown, Quote Me For dropdown
Tab 3 – Final Steps: Co-listing Agent toggle & fields, Referral Source, Comments
Tab 4 – Terms: Cancellation policy, no AMEX note, 3% processing fee, consent checkboxes, Submit Order button, and validation errors

subtasks.name: 5.5 - About, Catalogs, Pricing & Services
subtasks.description: About platform screen, service catalog with 4 category tabs, multi-state pricing grids, regional office contact directory, and support chat placeholder.

subtasks.name: 5.5.1 - About Main Screen
subtasks.description: FPV overview branding card. Main CTA buttons: Open Website (external link), Services Catalog (routes to catalog), Contact Regional Office (opens state directory). App support section: Account profile, Native ordering, Scan tools. Chat card with Coming Soon indicator.

subtasks.name: 5.5.2.B - Marketable Tab
subtasks.description: Most Requested section: Residential Floor Plans, Site Plans, Commercial Floor Plans cards. Each card has: title, overview text, Good For checklist, View on Website button. Residential Floor Plans: MLS listings, print flyers, marketing packets use-cases.

subtasks.name: 5.5.2.C - As-Built Tab
subtasks.description: Services: As-Built Floor Plans (engineering blueprints with wall thickness and plumbing), Exterior Elevations (building facades), Interior Elevations (kitchen cabinets, fireplace, shelving), Building Sections (cross-section framing views), Roof Plans (slopes, drainage, chimney).

subtasks.name: 5.5.2.D - 3D & Media Tab
subtasks.description: Services: 3D Walk-Through Tours (virtual Matterport models), 3D Renderings (exterior + interior high-res), 3D Animations (fly-over videos + interior walkthrough clips), Matterport Video (exported dollhouse clips).

subtasks.name: 5.5.2.E - Add-Ons Tab
subtasks.description: Add-on options: Exterior Add-On (backyard elevations/site plans), Square Footage Calculations (formal certificates), Furniture Add-On (interior furniture symbols overlay), CAD File Release (layered DWG file delivery), 2D/3D Plans From Scan (generated from raw Matterport data). Need Help section with Pricing and Website redirect buttons.

subtasks.name: 5.5.3.B - Plans
subtasks.description: State-selectable pricing (Arizona base). B&W and Color price brackets by sq ft: 0-1000sqft 50/00, 1001-2000sqft 00/50, 2001-3000sqft 50/00, 3001-4000sqft 00/50. 4000+ sqft: custom quote required.

subtasks.name: 5.5.3.C - From Existing
subtasks.description: Conversion pricing for old/paper blueprints to layered AutoCAD+PDF. Base flat fee starting at 00. Incremental size brackets and revision review rates.

subtasks.name: 5.5.3.D - Rendering
subtasks.description: Visual rendering prices: 3D Exterior Rendering, 3D Interior Rendering (room/material), Fly-Over Animation Video (aerial), Interior Walk-Thru Animation Video. Custom quote via email CAD/PDF submission note.

subtasks.name: 5.5.3.E - MatterPort
subtasks.description: Base services by sq ft: 0-1000sqft 50, 1001-2000sqft 00, 2001-3000sqft 50, 3001-4000sqft 00, 5000+sqft 50/hr. Add-On services: Outdoor Scans 00/hr, Detached Scan 0, Link Renewal 5/3mo, 2D Plan From Scan 50, 2D Color Plan 00, 3D Color Plan 50, Matterport Video below 1000sqft 25, above 1000sqft +0/1000sqft.

subtasks.name: 5.5.4 - FVP Contact List (15+ States)
subtasks.description: State-by-state office directory. California (SF, LA, SD), Arizona (Phoenix), Texas (Austin, Houston), Florida (Miami), Washington DC, Nevada (Reno/Tahoe), Illinois (Chicago), New York (NYC), New Jersey, Colorado (Denver), Connecticut, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Georgia (Atlanta), South Carolina (Charleston), Maryland, Virginia, Massachusetts (Boston), Washington (Seattle). Plus Other category.

subtasks.name: 5.5.5 - (Not Ready) Live Chat Module Placeholder Screen
subtasks.description: Chat placeholder page with Coming Soon status badge. Displays: future in-app live support connection queue description. Chat card with chevron arrow in About main screen.

subtasks.name: PHASE 6 — Prototype & Mid-Review
subtasks.description: Interactive prototype build and mid-project client presentation. Second revision round based on client feedback on the full design.

subtasks.name: 6.1 - Interactive Prototype Build
subtasks.description: Link all screens into a clickable prototype covering primary user flows: login, home, order creation wizard, order details, account settings, and about/services.

subtasks.name: 6.2 - [REVISION 2] Mid-Project Client Presentation & Feedback
subtasks.description: Present the full prototype to the client. Walk through all main flows. Collect prioritised feedback and document all requested changes.

subtasks.name: 6.3 - Screen Revisions Based on Mid-Review Feedback
subtasks.description: Apply all changes from the mid-review. Update screens, components and prototype links accordingly.

subtasks.name: PHASE 7 — Final Review, Handoff, Other services
subtasks.description: Final polish pass, client sign-off presentation, last-round revisions, and complete developer handoff package.

subtasks.name: 7.1 - Final Design Polish & Consistency Check
subtasks.description: Full pass over every screen: spacing consistency, type hierarchy, component alignment, colour correctness, and edge case state coverage.

subtasks.name: 7.3 - Selective Interaction Prototypes
subtasks.description: Targeted interactive prototypes created only where necessary to demonstrate specific UX behaviors, component interactions, transitions, navigation logic, or complex user flows. This does not represent a full app prototype, but rather focused interaction simulations used to improve clarity, usability, and implementation direction during the design process.

subtasks.name: 7.4 - [REVISION 3] Final Client Presentation
subtasks.description: Present polished design to client for final approval. Collect last feedback to proceed to handoff.

subtasks.name: 7.5 - Figma Project Delivery
subtasks.description: Ongoing organization and maintenance of the Figma project structure throughout the design process. This includes consistent page organization, layer and component naming, file cleanup, asset export preparation, and overall design system structure management to ensure the project remains scalable, readable, and development-friendly during all stages of production and final delivery.

subtasks.name: 7.6 - Overall Feedback & Refinement Estimation
subtasks.description: Estimated time allocation for ongoing client reviews, feedback discussions, approval checkpoints, revision requests, and iterative refinements throughout the design process. Since the scope and frequency of revisions may vary during production, this task represents the estimated time reserved for ongoing communication and UX/UI iteration workflows.
